reviews.review_msg: Not a great experience as one who prefers not to self-teach the material for an in-person course. Difficult to follow along 200-slide ppts, with 90% being Excel screenshots (not explained well, either). Exams don't fully match assigned practice. 1 hour group-led seminars on sustainability take up time that could be for doing practice questions.

Claims that the slides are "for my teaching and not your learning". She doesn't teach concepts she just points at excel screenshots with minimal explanations. 1 hour seminar on sustainability at the end of every class (mandatory attendance). Midterm and final are extremely difficult and content doesn't match practice/assigned questions.

The course content isn't difficult on its own, but the professor makes it much harder than it needs to be. Her explanations are unclear and she doesn't add value in lectures. Exams cover material or question types we never saw in class or homework. If you're someone who needs clear instruction and fair exams, avoid this professor.

Don't take this class with her, just wait for a good prof. The midterm was alright but the final was like 30 pages long and so confusing not because the concepts are difficult, rather it was due to her not being able to word questions properly.

In my personal opinion, one of the least organized professors I have had for accounting. Lectures are extremely hard to follow and she provides excel solutions to practise questions which make it very hard to understand (much harder then it should be.)

I have taken a couple of accounting classes with her. She provides a lot of resources that requires tons of reading and prep but she is very caring and helpful both inside and outside the classroom. Getting a good grade is definitely possible if you spend the time and effort she expects.
She has really long slides with excel screenshot of examples. The lectures are not really helpful, you will need a lot of self study. Fair exams but pretty long.

She's very nice but her exams were somewhat difficult and in class just reads off slides (which are nothing like the exams). There's a huge group project and another one as well but its related to sustainability and balanced scorecard concepts when really, I'd prefer to just do accounting practice! I felt like I only learned fluff in this class.

Super chill prof, exams are straight forward. Just do the practice questions he assigns along with reviewing the in class exercises and you'll be good. Lectures are okay, however he does make them entertaining by making jokes.

Nice person, but very unorganized. We had a presentation worth 35% and all we had to go off was previous years examples, no rubric. Further instructions were added 24 hours before presenting. He was also on his phone during every presentation and told us that in previous years the highest mark went to the nicest looking PowerPoint and not content.

Literally better off not going to lecture. He constantly goes off on rants that are vaguely related to the course and doesn't teach you anything that is going to be on the tests. He gives practice exams at least.

Ray is a good prof. He focuses on teaching the core concepts of the course instead of giving long lectures on theory. Provides with homework and practice questions which are similar to exam questions. Textbook not required. Overall a solid choice.

I took BUS 322 in Fall and found Ray is a fair professor. He does not walk you through step-by-step for practice questions cause you can learn ALL calculations in his TA's notes which is given. Be careful, he drops hints in lecture which essay questions will be on exams. Final is harder than midterm. Group is self-construced, wisely choose members.

DON'T TAKE HIS CLASS. WORST PROF I'VE EVER SEEN. Unorganized at all. You dont need him to learn the course, and you pay the tuition for self-studying. No one else would be worse than him. If you see his name when enrolling coursestake the course next time, please. I dont comment on his grading, but he is definitely a bad lecturer.

Safia is a super nice prof! The content is not super difficult compared to the other 300-level acc courses. The midterm is quite easy, but final was a bit more difficult. Make sure to do the questions and practice exams she provides and you will be set! Find a good group for the presentation, and ask clarifying questions. Curve is relatively high.
Good Professor.
funny and gives good examples that back up the material. is giving us a pizza party on the last day. shoutout ray zhang

A bit disorganized, but he is very effective at synthesizing the textbook information and providing lots of relevant examples during lectures. if you take him, go to class! he's a pretty chill dude too.

She is awful and should not be teaching at a Post secondary level
She is difficult to understand due to her strong accent. When asked a question, she never knows the answers.
Avoid her if possible. You are better off reading your text book that attending her classes as she just reads her slides that gets forn the text book.  You will be on your own

the worst prof  i have ever had in sfu. she reads the slides, without adding any extra explanation. regret for the whole semester because i need to study all the materials by myself. and she is never available in her office hour.
She read slides, nothing else. Her accent is really heavy, and she could not answer questions properly because her English is terrible.

Prof gets nervous, hes nice and tries to help you out as much as he can but material is very dry and lectures are boring (as are most managerial classes). Midterm is more quantitative and final qualitative. textbook is useful if you dont pay attention.
pretty decent prof, a little boring at times but very clear and thorough... maybe too thorough

Okay, guys. If you take this class with him, be prepared. He fills his lectures with "useless" details about garbage... doesn't even have a CMA; even if you have an MBA you shouldn't be teaching an upper level manag. acct course. Also -- DOESN'T USE SLIDESHOW FEATURE ON POWERPOINT. That tells you all you need to know right there.

I think I would eat rotten fruits and vegetables than be in this class. I have gained a new appreciation for my life after spending time in this lecture. However, she is a hilarious professor not because of her jokes but rather the way she talks and expects us not to fall asleep. One of the most passive aggressive profs ever. -Pyara G
DON'T Take her class pls!!!!!!

Incredibly kind professor! Although he reads from his slides during lectures, but his exams are fair — cheat sheets are allowed! For written questions (20%), copy down all sample problems from his slides; he only changes the numbers. But MC questions (80%) are brutally detailed and require thorough knowledge of the material. SO READ YOUR TEXTBOOK!
Weiming liu is very very good he explains things very well

He's a nice guy but he spoke to his TA in Chinese… His English isn't great so it can be hard to understand what he's talking about and he doesn't seem to have any pedagogical skills. The course also included too much content, you aren't able to actually learn intuitively and understand, hence the cheat sheet.

Such a genuinely nice guy who tries his best to make managerial accounting fun. The class is a lot of work but his tests are very fair and easier than the content covered in lecture. Definitely recommend!
Pretty good professor to study accounting with at Beedie!

Let's face it, managerial accounting is a fairly dry course, but Francisco did a great job in making the class interesting through case studies and presentations as well as examples from his career past. The exams were fair and I felt well prepared. This is a professor that wants you to succeed and I'm glad I took the course with him!
REALLY NICE GUY, ALTHOUGH HAVE ACCENT, BUT HE IS REALLY NICE!FOCUS ON HIS NOTES, AND MAKE SURE YOU DO ALL THE HOMEWORK, THEN YOU WILL BE GOOD IN THIS CLASS! PRETTY FAIR EXAMS~

He teaches everything from the book. he doesn't make his own slides and uses the slides that comes with the book. Class is not hard, but boring.U will not learn anything in his class. Avoid at any cost.

Very disappointed with this professor. Does not have a helpful attitude towards students and is extremely unclear with his expectations. He loves the subject (managerial accounting), but he makes the exams unreasonably difficult. If you wish to take this course will him, then good luck - you'll need it.

His classes are very boring. It is hard to understand him sometimes. During lectures and tutorial he just goes over notes, which are all posted, making attendance sort of useless. Multiple choice questions are on the tricky side.

He's a good person who cares about students. However, his exams are too difficult. they are very discouraging. even if you practice all the tutorial questions you can still do poorly on the exam... not recommended for 322.

He is a very fair professor. He is helpful when you ask him questions that are concise and clear, and easy to get a long. This class is hard due to content, but Kamlani tries his best to help us understand all the material. It was helpful having him as my TA. His tests are difficult, but you really need to push yourself to understand the material.

He must have realized the midterm was so difficult that he made the final very very much easier. The # of theory questions on the final made me feel like I was sitting thru an OB exam rather than an accounting one. 20min into the lectures, and you'll find yourself still staring at the 1st ppt slide.

I thought the midterm was insanely hard and poorly worded, but the final was much more fair. Lectures were dull/unclear and I stopped attending, my friends detailed notes from Professor Spector was a must.
